pattern: Recomputing single-source shortest distances from scratch on every query keeps a directed weighted graph correct even as edges are added between queries, trading a per-query relaxation pass for the complexity of maintaining a live shortest-path index.
hints: Precomputing all-pairs distances once won't survive a later addEdge, and there are too few nodes to worry about an expensive per-query recomputation — recompute single-source distances from node1 fresh each time shortestPath is called.
A directed edge only relaxes distances in one direction; walk outward from node1 relaxing every outgoing edge you know about, including ones inserted after construction.

Track a distance array seeded at infinity with node1 at 0, repeatedly pick the closest unfinished node and relax its outgoing edges until nothing improves, then read off node2 (or -1 if it never got a finite distance).

constraints.note: each row [from, to, cost]; 0<=from,to<=n-1, 1<=cost<=1000000, no self-loops or repeated (from,to) pairs at any point
[from, to, cost] for a single addEdge call

edge_inputs.description: sample sequence: two queries before any edge is added, then addEdge unlocks a cheaper route
single node, no edges: the only reachable pair is a node to itself, at cost 0
two nodes with no path, then addEdge connects them and the path becomes usable
asking for the path from a node to itself must return 0 even when cheaper-looking cycles exist through neighbors
a direct edge exists but a two-hop route is cheaper; the answer must pick the cheaper total, not the direct edge
edges are directed: an edge from a to b does not imply a route from b to a
edge cost at the maximum constraint value
a long directed chain forces the calculator to sum every hop rather than shortcut

stress.description: random small directed graphs with interleaved addEdge and shortestPath calls, no self-loops or duplicate (from,to) pairs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, 15)
    existing = set()
    edges = []
    initial = rng.randint(0, min(20, n * (n - 1)))
    tries = 0
    while len(edges) < initial and tries < initial * 20 + 20:
        tries += 1
        f = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        t = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if f == t or (f, t) in existing:
            continue
        c = rng.randint(1, 1000)
        existing.add((f, t))
        edges.append([f, t, c])
    ops = ['Graph']
    args = [[n, edges]]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        if rng.random() < 0.25:
            added = False
            tries2 = 0
            while not added and tries2 < 20:
                tries2 += 1
                f = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
                t = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
                if f == t or (f, t) in existing:
                    continue
                c = rng.randint(1, 1000)
                existing.add((f, t))
                ops.append('addEdge')
                args.append([[f, t, c]])
                added = True
            if not added:
                ops.append('shortestPath')
                args.append([rng.randint(0, n - 1), rng.randint(0, n - 1)])
        else:
            ops.append('shortestPath')
            args.append([rng.randint(0, n - 1), rng.randint(0, n - 1)])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: generator tracks the existing (from,to) pair set so it never proposes a self-loop or a repeated directed edge

complexity.time: O(n^2) per shortestPath, O(1) per addEdge
